channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Does GTA 6 Have GTA Online Or Multiplayer At Launch?
item.content:encoded:
Everyone is undoubtedly excited for Grand Theft Auto 6, but there’s a huge community out there dying for the next evolution of GTA Online, with many hoping that comes with GTA 6.

Vice City is making a comeback, and from what we’ve seen so far, it looks like the perfect playground for some multiplayer madness. More races, more mayhem, and more heists is what we’re all after, but will any of that even be available in GTA 6, especially at launch?

The answer might surprise you; find out below.

Does GTA 6 Have GTA Online Or Multiplayer At Launch?

Rockstar has confirmed that Grand Theft Auto 6 is a ‘single-player experience,’ with no mention of an online mode.

It’s fair to say that the announcement has caught plenty of fans off guard, as many expected an online mode akin to GTA Online to release alongside GTA 6. However, Rockstar has made it clear that, at least at launch, GTA 6 will be strictly enjoyed in a single-player capacity.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

It’s a damning verdict, and whilst it might be disheartening to hear, don’t lose hope just yet. Rockstar has followed a reliable release pattern with its prior games, and there’s a strong chance the same could be happening here…

Will GTA 6 Get GTA Online Or Multiplayer?

Rockstar is yet to confirm whether GTA 6 will get its own multiplayer experience, but judging by previous releases, it’s incredibly likely.

When it came to GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2, both titles received their respective online game modes shortly after their initial release, giving gamers time to explore the open world and complete the campaign. Only then were the online doors opened to the community, and it’s a safe bet to assume the same could happen with GTA 6.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Claim Free Month Of GTA+ With GTA 6

Given the immense success and popularity of GTA Online, it’d be a monumental shock not to see an online mode eventually launch for GTA 6. Whether it releases as paid DLC, as a standalone title, or simply as a free add-on remains to be seen: just don’t expect to see it at launch—let Jason and Lucia have some of the spotlight first.

It is their story, after all, and the second trailer gave us a closer look at what that might look like. Give it another watch below whilst you wait for GTA 6.

Will you be playing GTA 6 Online, whenever it releases (assuming it does)? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 content, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All GTA 6 PlayStation-Exclusive Features—Every PS5 & PS5 Pro Bonus
item.content:encoded:
If you purchase Grand Theft Auto 6 on any PS5 console, you’re securing some fun extra features you won’t find on Xbox or eventually PC. They don’t define GTA 6, but they’re a fun inclusion nonetheless.

GTA 6 is coming to PlayStation and Xbox at launch. PC users are being made to wait, and there’s pretty much no chance of a Switch port.

That said, if you do buy GTA 6 with the intention of playing it on PS5 or your even more powerful PS5 Pro, PlayStation has revealed some extras.

Every PS5-Exclusive Feature For GTA 6

Sony has communicated that GTA 6 will have special Haptic feedback, 3D audio, fast load times, and speaker features exclusive to PlayStation, designed to enhance your GTA 6 experience.

Haptic Feedback

Exclusive Sony PlayStation 5 features have possibly not been utilized as much as they could have. PS5 first-party titles have benefitted from this: Returnal, Saros, and Marvel’s Spider-Man games, for example.

But GTA 6 will also do its best to bring you more reasons to enjoy the sixth mainline game on PlayStation.

  • More Insider Gaming: All GTA 6 Editions, Prices, Content & Pre-Order Bonuses

Here’s what the PS Store says about the feature:

‘Responsive vibrations from the DualSense wireless controller’s revolutionary haptic feedback will react to your choices and simulate environmental factors all across the sprawling state of Leonida and modern-day Vice City.’

It’s quite vague, and a Sony Blog elaborated: ‘The controller’s haptic feedback offers responsive vibrations, while its adaptive triggers provide dynamic resistance. The controller’s integrated speaker also adds another dimension to key moments and interactions, with sound effects from the controller enhanced by haptic feedback.’

This could mean proper kickback from car crashes, gunfire, explosions, and more.

Tempest 3D Audio

3D audio was one of the core selling points in the build-up to the PS5’s launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Claim Free Month Of GTA+ With GTA 6

The Tempest 3D audio gets nice use in GTA 6:

‘Immerse yourself in Leonida’s distinct soundscape with the enhanced and highly accurate audio positioning of Tempest 3D Audio, making each unique situation come alive.’

The blog added: ‘With Tempest 3D AudioTech, you can surround yourself in the distinct soundscapes of Leonida. From the streets of Vice City to the moments that unfold all across the state, this highly accurate audio positioning enhances your perception, helping to bring this world to life around you in unmatched ways.’

Rockstar Games is as focused on attention to detail as anyone, and playing GTA 6 through headphones will be ridiculously immersive.

Integrated Speaker

This ties back into haptic feedback, but the PlayStation DualSense will probably have phone calls playing out on your controller, and relay back sound in new ways:

‘The DualSense wireless controller’s integrated speaker also offers an extra dimension with featured occurrences and interactions accentuated by remarkable sound effects from the controller – often enhanced by immersive haptic feedback.’

Fast Load Times

Now, Sony is pushing the narrative that GTA 6 plays ‘Best on PS5’.

This is helped by GTA 6 being PS5 Pro Enhanced, as well as the special features I’ve outlined already. But playing GTA on PS5 means practically no loading screens on PS5.

Does this mean Xbox Series X owners won’t get fast loading times? I wouldn’t think so, the XSX is a powerful console in its own right, and the same likely applies to Microsoft’s biggest hardware beast.

On the other hand, Sony is happily advertising that you won’t spend much time dithering in loading screens:

‘Grand Theft Auto VI also leverages the PS5’s ultra-high speed SSD, enabling you to experience the expansive world of Leonida with near-instant load times.’

Are you buying GTA 6 on PlayStation, Xbox, or PC, once it’s eventually out? Let us know your guesses through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the interesting find that GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ with no mention of GTA Online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: What Time Can You Pre-Order GTA 6 In US & UK? GTA 6 Pre-Order Start Time
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is ready to take over the gaming world, as it’s almost time to secure your copy, but if you’re in the US and the UK, you’re likely still waiting.

Pre-orders are already live in places like New Zealand, as Rockstar has opted for a regional approach, and it’s got plenty of fans wondering when it’s their turn to empty their wallets.

Worry not, as we have it all explained below. Here’s when GTA 6 pre-orders go live in the US and the UK, including exact start times.

GTA 6 Pre-Order Release Date

Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders begin on June 25, 2026, almost five months before the game launches on November 19.

When it comes to pre-orders, you’ll find that there are two editions to pick from: the Standard Edition and the more luxurious Ultimate Edition, which comes packed with exclusive cosmetics, cars and story content.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

That said, GTA 6 is only the second game in history to launch for $80, with Nintendo’s Mario Kart World setting the tone last Summer. It’s still a high price, but, depending on how you look at it, the Ultimate Edition only costs an extra $20, bringing the overall price to $100.

Pre-orders are available on both the digital stores for the PlayStation 5 and the XBOX Series X|S, or from any standard local retailer, though you won’t have much luck with a physical edition, since it’ll be missing a disc. It’s a sad moment for physical media fans, but if you’re still determined to pre-order the game, here’s exactly when you can do so.

GTA 6 Exact Pre-Order Start Times

Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders will go live at 12 AM local time on June 25, in both the UK and the US.

That means those of you in the UK will get to pre-order the game slightly earlier than anyone in the US, especially compared to the folk on the West Coast.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Claim Free Month Of GTA+ With GTA 6

Hopefully, that short delay shouldn’t impact you too much; after all, you’ve still got five months to mull over whether you want to pre-order or not before the game releases! Still, if you want to pre-purchase the game and put it on the back of your mind, the option becomes available at 12 AM your time, so you might have a late night ahead of you!

It’s undeniably an exciting time to be a GTA fan, so why not mull over the second trailer another time while we wait for the third one to drop? Check it out below:

Will you be pre-ordering GTA 6? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 content, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: Kadokawa CEO Being Pushed Out Over Elden Ring ‘Failure’
item.content:encoded:
A new report reveals that shareholders want Kadokawa Group’s CEO to resign over failure to capitalize on Elden Ring profits.

Elden Ring is one of the most popular souls-like games available, boasting millions of sales across various platforms. The devs have also churned out spinoff titles and even confirmed a spinoff movie, establishing the franchise. However, despite its remarkable success, the game has not translated into significant profits for the company, leading to accusations against the CEO.

A recent report by Reuters reveals that Kadokawa Group, the parent company of Elden Ring dev FromSoftware, is currently facing pressure from shareholders who are calling for the resignation of CEO Takeshi Natsuno. While Natsuno remains in his position for now, a conflict is anticipated at the annual general meeting (AGM) scheduled for this Wednesday.

Oasis Management, based in Hong Kong, is leading the resistance as the largest shareholder in Kadokawa, having acquired a 13.76% stake in the company. They have accused CEO Natsuno of failing to take advantage of the success of Elden Ring. Kadokawa’s return on equity decreased significantly, falling from 9.4% in 2022 to only 0.5% last year. Additionally, Oasis has claimed that there has been “material profit leakage” due to Kadokawa managing publishing in Japan while Bandai handles overseas publishing.

  • More Insider Gaming: Elden Ring Tarnished Edition Nintendo Switch 2 Release Date Announced

Oasis is not the only stakeholder seeking the removal of the Kadokawa CEO due to Elden Ring’s failure. A proxy report from Institutional Shareholder Services indicates, “While it may take time to find a replacement for Natsuno, this is a challenge worth accepting.” Additionally, another proxy advisor, Glass Lewis, suggests that shareholders should back Oasis’s proposal.

What are your thoughts on shareholders wanting Kadokawa’s CEO to resign over Elden Ring’s failure? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about how you can pre-order GTA 6 right now in New Zealand. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Baddie Restaurant RNG Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox My Baddie Restaurant monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including and much more.

My Baddie Restaurant is a restaurant management game on Roblox that throws players into the chaotic world of running their own dining establishment with quirky characters called Baddies. The game begins with a humorous twist—your restaurant has completely collapsed, leaving you with nothing but debt and determination. From this rocky start, players must rebuild their culinary empire by collecting Baddies through a dice-spinning mechanic, then putting these characters to work cooking dishes and serving customers to generate income.

The core gameplay revolves around strategic management and collection mechanics. Players spin dice to unlock new Baddies, with rarer variants offering faster cooking speeds and better customer tips. The game features progression systems including rebirth mechanics that unlock additional content and rewards, with certain codes requiring specific rebirth levels to redeem.

Since its release, My Baddie Restaurant has carved out its niche in the Roblox restaurant tycoon genre. While grinding through gameplay naturally provides resources for expansion, redeeming codes offers players a convenient shortcut to obtain extra spins and special potions that would otherwise require significant time investment or premium currency purchases.

Active Baddie Restaurant RNG Codes

  • BADDIE: 1 Spin (requires Rebirth level 3)
  • NewFloorsYay: 5 Spins (requires Rebirth level 13)
  • OneMoreSpin: 10 Spins

Expired Baddie Restaurant RNG Codes

  • BUTIMASTAR
  • RELEASE

How To Redeem Baddie Restaurant RNG Codes

Redeeming codes for My Baddie Restaurant is rather simple, much like most Roblox games. Here’s how:

  1. Launch My Baddie Restaurant in Roblox
  2. Find the Store on the far left of the screen
  3. Click Store, scroll down to the codes section and find the text box
  4. Type or paste a working code into the text box
  5. Click on “redeem” to claim your freebie

Where To Get more Baddie Restaurant RNG Codes

Finding active My Baddie Restaurant codes requires knowing where the developers actually communicate with their community. The game’s official Discord server is the primary hub where codes get announced, and YumYum! posts them directly in dedicated channels, often bundled with announcements about new features or gameplay adjustments. Joining the Discord gives you direct access to these announcements and lets you interact with fellow restaurant managers who share code discoveries they’ve found across different platforms.

Social media platforms serve as secondary distribution points for promotional codes. The developers use these channels to reward the community during special occasions; whether celebrating the game reaching popularity milestones, launching new Baddie types, or marking seasonal events. Codes typically appear without much advance notice and can vanish just as quickly, making it frustrating to stay on top of every potential source. Missing a code window means losing out on free spins or potions that could significantly boost your restaurant’s growth.

There’s a simpler approach, though. Bookmark this article and return periodically rather than juggling multiple social feeds and community servers. We update this resource whenever new codes become available, and we remove dead codes promptly so you’re only seeing currently functional redemptions.

Enjoying our Roblox games codes coverage? Check out the codes for popular games like Steal A Brainrot, Radiant Residents, and Fix It Up on Insider Gaming!

Interested in the latest developments in the world of gaming and esports? Then join the Insider Gaming Discord server and engage with the passionate community.
